title: Veterans' Rehabilitation and Education Amendments of 1980
fullText:
Veterans' Rehabilitation and Education Amendments of 1980 (Public Law 96-466).
An Act to amend title 38, United States Code, to revise the vocational rehabilitation program, to make improvements in the educational assistance programs, and for other purposes.

TITLE I—VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION
This is the organic, foundational act that completely restructured Title 38 Chapter 31. It replaced the old post-WWII and Vietnam-era vocational rehabilitation system with the modern vocational rehabilitation and employment (VR&E) tracks, establishing the modern 12-year basic eligibility window, the 48-month entitlement cap, and the Independent Living track.
plainEnglish: The main law passed in 1980 that created the modern VR&E Chapter 31 program, setting the basic rules we use today (like the 12-year limit and the five training tracks).
veteranUse: The bedrock law for the entire Chapter 31 program. Explains the original intent of Congress to provide comprehensive services rather than just basic job training.
